featured.title: Pfand in Germany: Berlin Bottle Deposits Explained for Tourists
featured.label: Useful now
featured.summary: A simple tourist guide to bottle deposits in Berlin: what 25 cents means, where to return bottles and what to do if the machine rejects them.
featured.alt: A bottle return machine at an Edeka supermarket in Berlin, where tourists can return Pfand bottles and cans

posts.title: Tipping in Berlin: How Much to Tip in Restaurants, Taxis, Hotels and Walking Tours
posts.summary: Restaurants, bars, cafes, taxis, hotels, walking tours, card payments, cash tips and what to say when paying.
posts.alt: Cafe Moskau restaurant building in Berlin, used to illustrate tipping in Berlin restaurants and cafes

posts.title: Public Toilets in Berlin: What Tourists Actually Need to Know
posts.summary: Where to find public toilets in Berlin in 2026, what they cost, which are free, and the official app worth knowing.
posts.alt: Restroom sign with male and female symbols on a brick wall, pointing right. Green trees and glass buildings in the background.

posts.title: Why Is Berlin's Founding Year 1237? Cölln, Medieval Berlin and the City Before 1933
posts.summary: Berlin's official birthday comes from a 1237 document mentioning Cölln, not Berlin itself. Here is the older story most visitors miss.
posts.alt: St. Mary's Church in central Berlin with the TV Tower rising behind it
